text.title: Stakeholder Management Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed across deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: High-power resistor blindsides launch; team had them on Power/Interest grid as 'monitor'.
antipattern.root-cause: Grid lacked attitude column; resistor mistaken for neutral observer.
antipattern.fix: Add explicit attitude column (Supporter / Neutral / Resistor) and re-classify quarterly.

antipattern.symptom: Senior leader complains: 'nobody told me'; not on any owner's list.
antipattern.root-cause: Stakeholder row created without named owner; cadence drifted.
antipattern.fix: templates/stakeholder-lint.py rejects rows without owner + cadence; orphans must be deleted or assigned.

antipattern.symptom: All-stakeholder email triggers loud resistor reply-all; supporters disengage.
antipattern.fix: Tailor channels by attitude + power; reserve broadcasts for canonical metric rollups.

antipattern.symptom: Resistor goes silent for 3 weeks; reappears via executive sponsor block.
antipattern.root-cause: No re-engagement protocol; silence treated as acceptance.
antipattern.fix: Within 7 days of silence, schedule a 1:1 (not email) and log the outcome.
